# ghost_webhook (Resource)

Manages a Ghost webhook. Webhooks fire HTTP POST requests to a target URL when specific Ghost events occur (e.g. `post.published`, `member.added`).

See the [Ghost webhook docs](https://ghost.org/docs/webhooks/) for the full list of supported events.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "ghost_webhook" "deploy" {
  event      = "site.changed"
  target_url = "https://ci.example.com/hooks/ghost-deploy"
  name       = "Trigger deploy on site change"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `event` (String) Ghost event that triggers this webhook (e.g. `post.published`, `member.added`). Changing this forces a new resource.
- `target_url` (String) URL that receives the HTTP POST payload.

### Optional

- `api_version` (String) Ghost Admin API version for the webhook payload format (e.g. `v5.0`). Defaults to `v5.0`.
- `integration_id` (String) ID of the Ghost integration that owns this webhook. Required when using an integration API key. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `name` (String) Human-readable webhook name shown in the Ghost Admin.
- `secret` (String, Sensitive) Optional HMAC secret used to sign webhook payloads (`X-Ghost-Signature` header).

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Ghost-assigned webhook ID.
